title: The Tom Cruise Hall of Fame
format: Chris Ryan returns in person the week before 'Top Gun: Maverick' to help Sean and Amanda build a 10-film Tom Cruise Hall of Fame, after litigating the results of the 1992 movie draft and taste-testing the actual Cruise Christmas cake.

segments.heading: Tom Cruise, the last movie star
segments.note: biography and career arc before the induction

films.title: Risky Business
films.note: Sean's first exposure to Cruise was the dance, before he'd even seen A Few Good Men.

films.title: Top Gun
films.note: Framing device for the whole episode: a movie that 'tracks old' but that Maverick returns to as Cruise's defense of theatrical moviegoing.

films.title: A Few Good Men
films.note: Amanda's answer to 'when did you first see him': crystallizes his charisma and underdogness.

films.title: Jerry Maguire
films.note: The genre-blend example of peak, all-figured-out Cruise: comedy, drama, romance, sports movie in one.

films.title: Eyes Wide Shut
films.note: Called 'the inverse of the Tom Cruise persona': Kubrick riffing on his fame and getting a compelling, failing, cuckolded performance out of him.

films.title: Magnolia
films.note: Cited as proof Paul Thomas Anderson understands exactly what Cruise is good at, and lets him comment on his own image.

films.title: Collateral
films.note: Raised as evidence he can go against type (his only true villain performance) and still work.

segments.heading: The Tom Cruise Hall of Fame

films.title: Risky Business
films.note: The one nobody argued about: 'it's got to be in.'

films.title: Top Gun
films.note: Unanimous green from the start: 'the greenest of greens.'

films.title: Born on the Fourth of July
films.note: Called his best performance; swapped in for Rain Man in the final round of cuts.

films.title: A Few Good Men
films.note: 'Legendary' status on the show; the whisper-then-yell Nicholson cross-examination singled out as one of his best-directed scenes.

films.title: Mission: Impossible
films.note: The first film in the franchise and the introduction of Ethan Hunt; kept as the franchise's one-to-three-era representative.

films.title: Jerry Maguire
films.note: Oscar-nominated and never seriously at risk of getting cut.

films.title: Eyes Wide Shut
films.note: His 'acting year,' kept green alongside Magnolia despite what Sean calls his own Kubrick-and-PTA bias.

films.title: Magnolia
films.note: Called the number-one lock of the whole list; never seriously argued against.

films.title: Collateral
films.note: Amanda's pick, his only villain turn, survives the last cut alongside Fallout.

films.title: Mission: Impossible - Fallout
films.note: The post-2005 franchise slot, nearly swapped out for Rain Man; kept because, as Chris argues, it 'cements that Tom Cruise is back.'

segments.heading: The cutting room floor
segments.note: serious contenders that missed the final ten

picks.title: The Firm
picks.note: In and out of the ledger twice; a 'one lawyer only' rule against A Few Good Men ultimately sinks it.

picks.title: Rain Man
picks.note: His only Best Picture winner; argued back in late, then traded out for Born on the Fourth of July in the last round.

picks.title: Minority Report
picks.note: Called 'amazing,' but written off as more a Spielberg achievement than a Cruise one.

picks.title: Mission: Impossible - Ghost Protocol
picks.note: The Burj Khalifa set piece and the 'Hunt is back' movie; called good but loses out to Fallout as the franchise's later pick.

picks.title: Mission: Impossible - Rogue Nation
picks.note: Called 'great' and praised for introducing Rebecca Ferguson; still voted out against Fallout.

picks.title: The Mummy
picks.note: 'This movie is an abomination': Sean's only non-festival walkout, and the stalled attempt to make Cruise the Dark Universe's centerpiece.

picks.title: The Color of Money
picks.note: The nine-ball hustle set to Werewolves of London; goes green early, then loses a head-to-head with Collateral.

picks.title: Cocktail
picks.note: Nobody can decide about it: a proposed zodiac-killer cipher stands in for an actual color, and it never gets voted in.

picks.title: Days of Thunder
picks.note: Acknowledged as tapping the same vein as Top Gun and not pursued further.

picks.title: Far and Away
picks.note: An instant pass.

picks.title: Interview with the Vampire
picks.note: A villain turn as Lestat, called 'pretty bad' on rewatch; an instant pass.

picks.title: Mission: Impossible II
picks.note: John Woo's entry; not in, despite admiration for the swing of hiring him.

picks.title: Vanilla Sky
picks.note: The Cameron Crowe remake of Abre los Ojos; called admirable and interesting but not Hall of Fame material.

picks.title: The Last Samurai
picks.note: Rewatched for the first time in 20 years and judged 'a bit of a slog.'

picks.title: War of the Worlds
picks.note: Defended as an underrated blockbuster experience, but voted out as more a Spielberg movie than a Cruise one.

picks.title: Mission: Impossible III
picks.note: Called Philip Seymour Hoffman's movie more than his, and the fourth-best entry in the franchise.

picks.title: Lions for Lambs
picks.note: The Redford-directed political drama; an instant pass.

picks.title: Tropic Thunder
picks.note: The glorified-cameo studio-exec parody; charming to some, 'trying too hard' to others, cut in a later trim.

picks.title: Valkyrie
picks.note: Voted red immediately despite a stacked supporting cast.

picks.title: Knight and Day
picks.note: The James Mangold action comedy with Cameron Diaz; a flop with a good premise that doesn't hang together.

picks.title: Jack Reacher
picks.note: McQuarrie's directorial debut with Cruise; left off given how many other cuts were already needed.

picks.title: Rock of Ages
picks.note: Dismissed instantly and 'seriously' disliked.

picks.title: Oblivion
picks.note: Rewatched to prep for Maverick and called better than expected, but only ever yellow.

picks.title: Edge of Tomorrow
picks.note: A five-star movie for Sean and Chris and the last one cut, dropped because it doesn't carry the 'Cruise reinvented himself' argument that Fallout does.

picks.title: All the Right Moves
picks.note: A Bill Simmons favorite; acknowledged with love and still left out.

picks.title: Legend
picks.note: Arguably Ridley Scott's worst movie, kept alive only as a sentimental pick before being voted out.

picks.title: Taps
picks.note: Rewatched for the first time since the early '90s; a self-consciously 'actorly' performance in a mode Cruise would soon abandon completely.

picks.title: The Outsiders
picks.note: Ninth-billed in the ensemble; obviously not a contender.

picks.title: Losin' It
picks.note: A teen sex comedy from the same year as Risky Business; mentioned only in passing.

films: Mission: Impossible - Dead Reckoning Part One

Born on the Fourth of July.title: Born on the Fourth of July

Mission: Impossible - Dead Reckoning Par.title: Mission: Impossible - Dead Reckoning Part One

Birdman.title: Birdman or (The Unexpected Virtue of Ignorance)
